title: Tantalizing Normandy Runner Beans Tarte Tatin
description: A savory, plant‑based tarte tatin featuring tender runner beans, caramelized apples, and delicate bean spheres, perfect for midnight cravings.
instructions: Trim the ends of the runner beans and blanch them in salted boiling water for 2 minutes, then shock in ice water.
In a skillet, melt vegan butter with olive oil, then sauté the sliced onion and minced garlic until translucent.
Add the blanched beans, maple syrup, sea salt, and thyme leaves to the skillet, cooking for 5 minutes until the mixture caramelizes slightly.
Meanwhile, preheat the oven to 200°C and lightly grease a 20cm cast‑iron skillet.
Arrange the thin apple slices over the bean mixture in a single layer, then spoon the caramelized bean mixture over the apples.
Place the thawed puff pastry sheet over the apples, tucking the edges around the filling inside the skillet.
Bake for 25 minutes until the pastry is golden brown and the apples are soft.
While the tarte tatin bakes, prepare the spherification by stirring 2 Tbsp sodium alginate into 1 L of cold water, then allowing it to sit for 10 minutes.
Strain the alginate solution and carefully drop the mixture into a 1 L calcium lactate bath to form orange‑colored bean spheres, letting them set for 3 minutes before removing and rinsing.
Once the tarte tatin is finished, let it cool for 5 minutes, then invert onto a serving plate and garnish with the bean spheres.
